title: Rigged Liouville space formulation for quasi-Hermitian Liouville operators
abstract: We discuss a super bra-ket formalism for quasi-Hermitian Liouvillian operators within the framework of rigged Hilbert spaces (RHS). An RHS in terms of the Liouville space, referred to as a rigged Liouville space (RLS), is reconstructed by exploiting the mathematical fact that the space of Hilbert-Schmidt operators is unitarily equivalent to the tensor product of Hilbert spaces. The obtained RLS endows a rigorous foundation of the construction for the super bra-ket and for the spectral decompositions of both Hermitian and quasi-Hermitian Liouville operators, which are characterized by the generalized eigenvectors in the dual spaces. Furthermore, within this framework, the non-Hermitian Liouvillian operator and its adjoint can be constructed symmetrically, with their symmetric structure preserved. As an application of our RLS methodology, we examine the Liouville operators corresponding to Hermitian and non-Hermitian harmonic oscillators and elucidate the differences between their spectral decomposition forms.
summary: This paper develops a rigorous mathematical framework called Rigged Liouville Space (RLS) to handle the spectral decomposition of quasi-Hermitian Liouville operators. It addresses a fundamental mathematical inconsistency regarding the adjoint of composite non-Hermitian operators in standard Hilbert space. By using Rigged Hilbert Spaces, the authors show that the symmetry of the Liouvillian and its adjoint can be preserved. The framework is demonstrated using the Swanson Hamiltonian, showing how the expansion transitions from an orthonormal basis to a bi-orthogonal system. This work provides a solid foundation for the super bra-ket formalism used in open quantum systems.
main_problem: Establishing a rigorous mathematical foundation for the super bra-ket formalism and spectral decomposition of quasi-Hermitian Liouville operators within a Rigged Liouville Space (RLS) framework.
main_result: The authors successfully constructed a Rigged Liouville Space that allows for a symmetric construction of non-Hermitian Liouvillian operators and their adjoints, resolving previous mathematical inconsistencies in the adjoint relation of composite systems.
method: The study utilizes the framework of Rigged Hilbert Spaces (RHS) and unitary transformations to reconstruct a Rigged Liouville Space (RLS) by exploiting the equivalence between Hilbert-Schmidt operators and tensor products of Hilbert spaces.
model_or_system: The framework is applied to $\eta$-quasi-Hermitian systems, specifically focusing on the Lindblad master equation for open quantum systems and the Swanson Hamiltonian (a non-Hermitian PT-symmetric harmonic oscillator).
key_observables: Spectral expansion coefficients, super bra-ket vectors, and the bi-orthogonal basis for the Liouvillian operator.
important_parameters: The metric operator $\eta$ (or $\zeta$), the conjugation operator $C$, and the parameter regime $\omega^2 - 4\alpha\beta > 0$ for the Swanson oscillator.
main_assumptions: Assumes the existence of a continuous conjugation operator $C$ that commutes with the intertwining operator $\eta$, and that $\eta$ is a positive invertible operator.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from the general construction of the RLS (Section 2), to the spectral expansion of Hermitian Liouville operators (Section 3), and concludes with the extension to quasi-Hermitian operators and applications to the harmonic oscillator (Section 4).
why_interesting: This paper provides the necessary mathematical rigor for treating the dynamics of non-Hermitian and open quantum systems, specifically resolving how to properly define adjoints and spectral expansions in the super operator formalism.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
